mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is above its recent trend.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:latest visible RSAFS value was 513356.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after FEDFUNDS crosses a restrictive threshold.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% or increases more than 0.75pp over 6 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest visible FEDFUNDS value was 2.42 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered restrictive-rate episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and slowdown in payroll growth signals deteriorating labor demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth falls below its 12-month average
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:latest visible UNRATE value was 3.6 as of t-1mo
fact:latest visible PAYEMS value was 151095.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-rise and PAYEMS-slowdown trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative turn in industrial production growth signals weakening goods output and earnings pressure in cyclical sectors, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:latest visible INDPRO value was 109.1818 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contractions are not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than INDPRO expansions, or if the triggered return difference has the wrong sign in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and lower future construction-linked activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:latest visible HOUST value was 1139.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a non-rising policy rate signals a negative real-rate shock and future tightening risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is flat or lower over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:latest visible CPIAUCSL value was 254.958 as of t-1mo
fact:latest visible FEDFUNDS value was 2.42 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and non-rising FEDFUNDS interaction has nonnegative mean 20-day forward S&P 500 returns and no negative return spread versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment with continued payroll expansion signals a soft-landing labor regime that supports consumption and risk appetite, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 4.0% and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:latest visible UNRATE value was 3.6 as of t-1mo
fact:latest visible PAYEMS value was 151095.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the low-UNRATE and positive-PAYEMS regime is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if hit rate is below the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth raises expectations for aggregate earnings growth and lowers recession-risk premia, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above its trailing 4-quarter average
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
fact:latest visible GDPC1 value was 18912.326 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 acceleration episodes do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than GDPC1 deceleration episodes, or if Rank IC is negative over any 3-year rolling window.
